roles.security:309958: triggering alert: machine-account IPC$ lsarpc access that can resemble directory reconnaissance
roles.security:310058: the same machine account reads the SYSVOL root, consistent with Group Policy processing
roles.security:310068: machine-account read of the Default Domain Policy gpt.ini
roles.security:310069: machine-account read of the Default Domain Controllers Policy gpt.ini

metadata.security_proposition: The machine-account SMB and RPC burst is attacker-driven reconnaissance or lateral movement rather than routine domain-controller activity.

attack_category.category: Routine domain SMB (benign, resembles AD enumeration)
attack_category.note: RESEARCHER-INTENT: evidence_condition = COUNTER. A host repeatedly accesses IPC$\lsarpc plus SYSVOL\Policies gpt.ini over SMB (5145). The IPC$\lsarpc access pattern is the SAME one AD-enumeration tools (SharpHound/BloodHound, via LSAR) produce, so a naive 'SMB/AD enumeration' detection flags it. BUT the subject is the domain controller's OWN machine account (WIN-DC-TESTUSER-4$) performing routine LSA RPC and Group Policy (gpt.ini) processing — standard domain-controller infrastructure activity, not reconnaissance. The machine-account subject + SYSVOL/GPO targets are the benign counter-evidence. Calibration role = over-triage-prone (down-rank): a model must NOT flag routine machine-account domain SMB as AD enumeration / lateral movement. Do not leak to model.

tier.why_single_source: Single sensor (Security 5145 share-object-access), scoped to host win-dc-486's own SMB activity. The log's other host (win-host-576, a PsExec attack) is excluded via the per-source computer filter. Uses the sources[] format only to host-scope a 2-host log (record ids collide across hosts); it is a Tier-1 single-sensor case, not fusion.

boundary_definition: A0 = an IPC$\lsarpc access (309958) — the alarming, enumeration-resembling event.
EV = more IPC$\lsarpc accesses and SYSVOL gpt.ini (GPO) reads, all by the same machine account.
Scoped to win-dc-486; the log's other host (win-host-576, PsExec) is excluded.
The benign counter-evidence is intrinsic: the subject is the DC's machine account and the targets are lsarpc (LSA RPC) + SYSVOL Group Policy — routine domain operations.

main_alert_selection.selection_method: IPC$\lsarpc (LSAR) named-pipe access over SMB is a common 'AD enumeration' detection trigger.
main_alert_selection.selection_reason: It resembles SharpHound/BloodHound LSAR enumeration; the machine-account subject + GPO context are the benign counter.

event_groups.description: IPC$\lsarpc accesses by the DC machine account (LSA RPC, resembles AD enumeration).
SYSVOL root + Policies gpt.ini reads (routine Group Policy processing).

curation_notes.note: RESEARCHER-PRIVATE: COUNTER / over-triage-prone. IPC$\lsarpc access resembles AD enumeration, but the machine-account subject + SYSVOL GPO context make it routine domain infrastructure activity. The model must not over-flag.
This is a real SOC false-positive class: detections keyed on IPC$\lsarpc/samr fire on legitimate machine-account LSA RPC and GPO traffic.

review_required.ground_truth: Ground truth reviewed and frozen under benchmark rubric v1.1.
